unknown_field_policies.description: Reject unknown fields with structured diagnostic/refusal behavior.
Ignore only with a diagnostic or evidence note.
Preserve unknown fields during read/write roundtrip.
Allow unknown fields only under an explicit extension namespace or declaration.

required_field_policies.description: Missing field can use a declared default; silent defaults remain forbidden.
Missing field refuses with diagnostic/refusal output.

default_policies.description: Defaults must be declared explicitly in schema/policy.
Default value is defined by the schema and visible to consumers.
Default value is introduced only by an explicit migration.

canonical_serialization_policies.description: Canonical JSON uses UTF-8, sorted keys, and normalized line endings.
Canonical TOML uses normalized formatting when declared.
Binary canonical encoding is declared and byte-exact.
Canonical serialization policy is not yet stable.

migration_policies.description: No migration is available or required.
Consumers must run a declared migration before use.
Older form can be read without mutating source.
Incompatible form must refuse with diagnostic output.
Policy is not yet mature; stable promotion is blocked.

refusal_policies.description: Refusal must include diagnostic code or evidence reference.
Refusal must include a stable refusal code.
Refusal must be represented as structured result/error data.
